1.title: Three sons, one name
1.text: The three sons of Pahoran who contended for the judgment-seat were named Pahoran, Paanchi, and Pacumeni — the first bearing his father's own name. Their rivalry split the people into three factions and opened the way to the book's first political murder.

1.question: Why did a serious difficulty arise at the start of the fortieth year?
options: Pahoran had died and his sons contended for the judgment-seat

1.template: they sent forth one Kishkumen, even to the judgment-seat of {0}

1.question: Who led the Lamanite army, being a Nephite dissenter and descendant of Zarahemla?

1.template: the king of the Lamanites, whose name was {0}, who was the son of Ammoron

1.question: What happened to Pacumeni, the chief judge, when Coriantumr took Zarahemla?
options: He was smitten against the wall and died

1.title: The boldness that backfired
1.text: By marching straight through the center of the land instead of striking the borders, Coriantumr caught the Nephites off guard and took Zarahemla — but ended up surrounded on every side by Moronihah and Lehi; his own daring delivered the Lamanites into Nephite hands.

1.template: among the number who were slain {0} was also found

1.question: How did Moronihah treat the captured Lamanites after retaking Zarahemla?
options: He let them depart out of the land in peace
He sent them to the king

2.question: Who was chosen by the voice of the people to fill the judgment-seat?

2.title: The origin of the Gadianton robbers
2.text: Gadianton, 'exceedingly expert in many words' and cunning in crime, took over Kishkumen's band and promised his followers power. From him would come the name of the 'Gadianton robbers,' who would plague the Nephites for generations.

2.template: the servant of Helaman, as they were going forth unto the judgment-seat, did stab {0} even to the heart

2.question: What does the record warn that Gadianton would bring about?
options: The ruin, almost the entire destruction of the people of Nephi
The fall of Zarahemla to the Lamanites

3.question: Where did the many who left Zarahemla in the forty-sixth year go?
options: To the land northward, to inherit the land

3.template: therefore they did build houses of {0}, in the which they did dwell

3.title: Houses of cement in the north
3.text: Because timber was scarce in the land northward, the Nephites became expert in working cement and built houses and cities of cement. They let trees grow and shipped timber in — a rare glimpse of this people's engineering and trade.

3.question: Why does Mormon say he cannot include even a hundredth part of this people's doings?
options: The records and history were too many for this work
It was forbidden to write them

3.template: Helaman did fill the judgment-seat with justice and {0}

3.question: What names did Helaman give his two sons?
What happened to the church in the forty-ninth year, amid the peace?
options: There was great prosperity; tens of thousands were baptized

3.title: Pride in the church
3.text: The text draws a careful distinction: pride entered 'not into the church of God, but into the hearts of the people who professed to belong to the church of God.' That pride led to the persecution of the more humble brethren.

3.template: did wax stronger and stronger in their {0}, and firmer and firmer in the faith of Christ

3.question: What happened in the fifty-third year?
options: Helaman died and his son Nephi began to reign

4.template: they succeeded in obtaining possession of the land of {0}

4.question: Where were the Nephites and the armies of Moronihah driven?

4.title: The root of the defeat
4.text: Mormon lists what caused the Nephites' great loss: pride, riches, oppressing the poor, withholding food from the hungry and clothing from the naked, and mocking what was sacred. It was not the Lamanites but their own iniquity that weakened them.

4.question: Why were the Nephites left to their own strength and did not prosper?
options: Because of their great wickedness and boasting in their own strength

4.question: What did Moronihah do when he saw the people repent?
options: He led them to regain half of their lands

4.template: the Spirit of the Lord doth not dwell in {0} temples

4.question: What did the Nephites conclude about their weakness?
options: That they had grown weak because of their transgressions, in a few years

5.template: Nephi delivered up the judgment-seat to a man whose name was {0}

5.question: According to Helaman, through whom can man be saved?
options: Only through the atoning blood of Jesus Christ

5.template: it is upon the {0} of our Redeemer, who is Christ, the Son of God, that ye must build your foundation

5.title: 'Build upon the rock'
5.text: In the counsel his sons kept, Helaman teaches that it is upon the rock of the Redeemer, Christ, that one must build a foundation — 'a sure foundation, a foundation whereon if men build they cannot fall,' though winds and storms beat upon them.

5.template: there were eight thousand of the Lamanites who were in the land of Zarahemla and round about baptized unto repentance

5.title: The same prison
5.text: Nephi and Lehi were cast into the very prison where, generations earlier, the servants of king Limhi had imprisoned Ammon and his brethren — an echo of the missionary history of the sons of Mosiah among the Lamanites.

5.question: What happened to Nephi and Lehi in prison when the Lamanites came to slay them?
options: They were encircled as if by fire, but were not burned
They were freed by an angel

5.template: it was a still voice of perfect mildness, as if it had been a {0}, and it did pierce even to the very soul

5.question: How many times did the voice come, calling to repentance?

5.template: And {0} said unto them: They do converse with the angels of God

5.title: Aminadab the dissenter
5.text: It was a Nephite dissenter named Aminadab who recognized the miracle: seeing the faces of Nephi and Lehi shine like angels, he told the multitude they were conversing with the angels of God and taught them to cry unto the voice until they had faith in Christ.

5.question: What did Aminadab say they must do to have the cloud of darkness removed?
options: Repent and cry unto the voice until they had faith in Christ

5.template: Peace, peace be unto you, because of your {0} in my Well Beloved, who was from the foundation of the world

5.question: What did the Lamanites who were convinced after these miracles do?
options: They laid down their weapons, their hatred, and the traditions of their fathers

6.question: What were the Lamanites like at the end of the sixty-second year?
options: The greater part became a righteous people, more righteous than the Nephites

6.title: The reversal
6.text: For the first time, the text declares that the righteousness of the Lamanites exceeded that of the Nephites — a reversal of the traditional roles, owing to the firmness and steadiness of the converted Lamanites in the faith.

6.template: the land north was called Mulek, which was after the son of {0}

6.question: What happened to Cezoram in the sixty-sixth year?
options: He was murdered by an unknown hand upon the judgment-seat

pairs.right: Went to preach in the land northward
Chief judge murdered by an unknown hand
Gave his name to the band of robbers and secret murderers

6.template: it is these secret oaths and covenants which {0} commanded his son should not go forth unto the world

6.question: Where did the secret oaths put into Gadianton's heart come from?
options: From the same being who plotted with Cain — the author of all sin
From the records delivered to Helaman
From the traditions of the Lamanites

6.title: The origin of the secret combinations
6.text: Mormon traces the secret oaths not to the records but to the 'same being' who tempted Adam and Eve, plotted with Cain against Abel, and inspired the tower of Babel — the author of all sin, who hands them down from generation to generation.

6.template: the Nephites did begin to dwindle in {0}, and grow in wickedness and abominations

6.question: What did the Gadianton robbers obtain among the Nephites?
options: Sole management of the government, trampling the poor and humble

6.question: How does Mormon describe the Nephites' state at the end of the sixty-eighth year?

7.question: Why did Nephi return from the land northward to Zarahemla?
options: The people of the north rejected all his words
He was driven out by the Lamanites

7.title: When the robbers govern
7.text: On his return, Nephi found the Gadianton robbers filling the judgment-seats — condemning the righteous for their righteousness and letting the guilty go free for their money. Corruption had seized the government itself.

7.template: that I could have had my days in the days when my father Nephi first came out of the land of {0}

7.title: The tower in the garden
7.text: Nephi was pouring out his soul to God upon a tower in his garden, by the highway leading to the chief market of Zarahemla. Passersby saw him and gathered multitudes — and from that public lament came his sermon of warning.

7.question: Why had Nephi gone up upon the tower in his garden?
options: To pour out his soul to God, sorrowing over the people's iniquities

7.template: ye will not hearken unto the voice of the good {0}

7.question: Why did Nephi say it would be better for the Lamanites than for the Nephites?
options: Because the Lamanites had not sinned against the great knowledge the Nephites received

7.template: And except ye repent ye shall {0}; yea, even your lands shall be taken from you

7.title: 'Not of myself'
7.text: Nephi closes by making clear he does not prophesy of himself: 'I know that these things are true because the Lord God has made them known unto me, therefore I testify that they shall be' — an affirmation that his message came from God.

8.question: Why were the judges of the Gadianton band angry with Nephi?
options: Because he spoke plainly about their secret works of darkness
Because he wanted to be king

8.title: Judges in the conspiracy
8.text: Those who tried to stir the people against Nephi were no ordinary opponents: they were judges who belonged to the secret band of Gadianton. Fearing the people, they dared not seize him — and Nephi used the chance to keep preaching.

8.question: What did some of the people argue in Nephi's defense?
options: That he was good and that only a prophet could testify such things
That he was the judge's kin

8.template: have ye not read that God gave power unto one man, even {0}, to smite upon the waters of the Red Sea
behold, the prophet {0} did testify boldly; for the which he was slain
Will ye say that the sons of Zedekiah were not slain, all except it were {0}?

8.question: What sign did Nephi give to prove he was a prophet?
options: That they would find the chief judge murdered by his own brother

9.title: The test of the five
9.text: Five men ran to the judgment-seat to test Nephi: if they found the judge dead as he had said, they would believe he was a prophet. They found him exactly so — but the people, not knowing, seized them as the murderers.

9.question: What did the five men find when they ran to the judgment-seat?
options: The chief judge fallen to the earth in his blood

9.question: Why did the people seize the five men?
options: They thought they were the murderers, smitten by God so they could not flee
Because they were of Gadianton's band

9.question: What did the five tell the judges?
options: That they ran, saw everything as Nephi had testified, and were imprisoned

9.template: Now tell us, and acknowledge thy fault; saying, Behold here is {0}; and also we will grant unto thee thy life

9.question: How did Nephi answer the judges who accused him?
options: He called them fools and stiffnecked and warned them of destruction

9.template: Behold I say unto you: Go to the house of {0}, who is the brother of Seezoram

9.title: Blood on the hem of the cloak
9.text: Nephi described every detail beforehand: the brother would deny the crime, but they would examine him and find blood on the skirts of his cloak. The sign was fulfilled exactly, and Seantum confessed the murder.

9.question: How was Nephi's sign fulfilled at the house of Seantum?
options: They found blood on his cloak and, in fear, he confessed the murder

9.question: What was the result after Seantum confessed?
options: The five and Nephi were set at liberty

9.template: Behold, he is a {0}, for except he was a god he could not know of all things

10.template: Blessed art thou, {0}, for those things which thou hast done

10.title: Reward for unwearyingness
10.text: The Lord blesses Nephi for having declared the word 'with such unwearyingness,' not fearing the people nor seeking his own life. It is this faithfulness that precedes the granting of a great power.

10.question: What power did the Lord grant Nephi?
options: The sealing power — what he bound on earth would be bound in heaven
The power to raise the dead
The power to reign as king
The power to walk on water

10.title: The sealing power
10.text: Nephi received the sealing power: whatever he bound or loosed on earth would be bound or loosed in heaven — and even power over the elements, to smite the earth with famine and pestilence. It is the first time this power is granted so explicitly in the Book of Mormon.

10.question: What did Nephi do upon receiving the word of the Lord?
options: He did not go home, but returned to the multitudes to declare the word

10.template: they could not take him to cast him into prison, for he was taken by the {0} and conveyed away

11.template: but O Lord, rather let there be a {0} in the land, to stir them up in remembrance of the Lord their God

11.title: A famine by request
11.text: Having the sealing power, Nephi does not ask for the destruction of the wicked, but for a famine — reckoning that the suffering would lead the people to remember God and repent, rather than keep slaying one another by the sword.

11.question: What did the people begin to do when they were about to perish by famine?
options: Remember the Lord and the words of Nephi

11.question: Why did Nephi cry unto the Lord to end the famine?
options: Because the people had repented and humbled themselves

11.question: What did the people do when the rain returned and the earth yielded?
options: They rejoiced and esteemed Nephi as a great prophet

11.question: What happened during the years of peace (seventy-seventh year)?
options: The church spread and the more part of the people, Nephites and Lamanites, belonged to it

11.template: they did search out all the secret plans of Gadianton; and thus they became robbers of {0}

11.title: The rebirth of the robbers
11.text: Though the Gadianton robbers had been swept away at Nephi's plea, they rise again: dissenters and Lamanites rediscover 'all the secret plans of Gadianton' and once more infest the mountains, defying even the armies of both Nephites and Lamanites.

11.question: What made the people remember the Lord again?
options: The great calamity brought by the robbers because of their iniquity

11.question: What happened in the eighty-fifth year?
options: The people grew prouder and more wicked, ripening for destruction

12.title: Mormon's meditation
12.text: This chapter is an authorial pause: Mormon breaks off the narrative to reflect on 'how false, and also the unsteadiness of the hearts of the children of men,' contrasting human frailty with the omnipotence of God's voice.

12.template: except the Lord doth chasten his people with many {0}
how great is the nothingness of the children of men; yea, even they are less than the {0} of the earth

12.question: What power does Mormon describe, saying the earth goes back to lengthen the day?

12.title: 'It is the earth that moveth'
12.text: In a striking detail for an ancient text, Mormon states that when it appears to man that the sun stands still, 'surely it is the earth that moveth and not the sun' — describing the earth's motion centuries before the heliocentric debate.

12.question: According to Mormon, what happens to a treasure the Lord curses?
options: No man shall ever find it

12.question: Whom does Mormon say shall be saved?
options: Those who repent and hearken to the voice of the Lord

12.template: they that have done evil shall have everlasting {0}

13.template: and {0} years pass not away save the sword of justice falleth upon this people

13.title: The prophet on the wall
13.text: Rejected and cast out of Zarahemla, Samuel — a Lamanite — did not give up: he returned and climbed the city wall to prophesy from there. It is a striking reversal, a righteous Lamanite preaching repentance to the wicked Nephites.

13.question: What did the Lord say he would do if the Nephites did not repent?
options: Take away his word and Spirit and turn the hearts of their brethren against them

13.question: Why was the great city of Zarahemla spared, according to Samuel?
options: For the sake of the righteous who were in it

13.template: And behold, a {0} shall come upon the land, saith the Lord of Hosts

13.question: Of what does Samuel accuse the people regarding the prophets?
options: Of casting them out, mocking them, stoning them, and slaying them

13.question: How does the people treat a man who says 'all is well' and speaks flattery?
options: They exalt him, give him gold and silver, and call him a prophet

13.template: the time cometh that he curseth your riches, that they become {0}, that ye cannot hold them

13.title: The slippery riches
13.text: Samuel prophesies a peculiar curse: because they set their hearts on riches, these would become 'slippery' — tools and swords would vanish, treasures would slip away. A memorable image of the emptiness of trusting in material goods.

13.question: What was the problem with the people's 'days of probation,' according to Samuel?
options: They procrastinated the day of salvation until it was too late

14.template: for {0} years more cometh, and behold, then cometh the Son of God to redeem

14.title: The sign of the birth
14.text: Samuel announces that on the night before Christ's birth there will be great lights in heaven — a day, a night, and a day with no darkness — and a new star will appear. These signs are fulfilled exactly in 3 Nephi.

14.question: What promise does Samuel make to those who believe on the Son of God?
What does Samuel say happens if we believe on the name of Christ?
options: We repent and gain a remission of sins through his merits

14.template: redeemeth all mankind from the first death—that {0} death

pairs.right: A day, a night, and a day with no darkness
Three days of darkness and great earthquakes

14.question: What truth does Samuel teach about agency?
options: That men are free to act for themselves and choose life or death
That only the righteous are free

14.template: he hath given unto you that ye might choose life or {0}

15.question: Why did the Lord chasten the people of Nephi, according to Samuel?
options: Because he loved them, they being a chosen people

15.title: Salvation came through the Nephites
15.text: Samuel acknowledges that salvation came to the Lamanites 'through the preaching of the Nephites' — and that for this the Lord prolonged their days. It is a Lamanite reminding the Nephites of the good they once did.

15.template: ye know also that they have {0} their weapons of war, and they fear to take them up

15.question: Which prophet does Samuel cite concerning the restoration of the Lamanites to the knowledge of the truth?

15.template: I will utterly destroy them, saith the Lord, because of their {0}

16.question: What happened when they cast stones and arrows at Samuel upon the wall?
options: The Spirit of the Lord shielded him and they could not hit him

16.title: Stones and arrows that missed
16.text: As Samuel prophesied on the wall, the unbelievers threw stones and shot arrows, but the Spirit of the Lord protected him — they could not hit him. The wonder itself led many more to believe his words.

16.template: Take this fellow and bind him, for behold he hath a {0}

16.question: What signs began to be fulfilled in the ninetieth year?
options: Great signs and wonders; angels appeared to wise men

16.question: What argument did the unbelievers use against Christ's coming?
options: That he should appear to them, not in a far land like Jerusalem

16.template: And thus ended the book of {0}, according to the record of Helaman and his sons

16.title: The end of the book of Helaman
16.text: The book closes in deep apostasy, with Satan ruling the people's hearts — but only five years remain until Christ's birth. It is the cliffhanger that opens the book of 3 Nephi and the fulfillment of Samuel's signs.
